prompt: Sensor readings are returning garbage values intermittently

turns.content:
<task>
Sensor readings are returning garbage values intermittently. The temperature sometimes shows values like -32768 or 65535.
</task>

arguments.reasoning: The values -32768 (0x8000) and 65535 (0xFFFF) are telltale signs of I2C communication failure. When I2C fails, the lines go high (all 1s = 0xFF). The problem is that read_temperature doesn't check the return value of i2c_read. If it returns -1 (error), the buffer still contains garbage/0xFF, and the code proceeds to use it. Fix: check i2c_read return value and handle errors.

arguments.message:
Found the issue. `read_temperature()` in sensor.c doesn't check the return value of `i2c_read()`. When the I2C bus fails (NACK, bus contention, timing issue), `i2c_read()` returns -1 but the code uses the uninitialized/garbage buffer anyway.

The values -32768 (0x8000) and 65535 (0xFFFF) are classic I2C failure signatures — the bus lines are pulled high, giving 0xFF bytes.

I'll add error checking and a retry mechanism.

arguments.summary: Root cause: i2c_read return value not checked. I2C failures produce garbage in buffer. Adding error handling with retry.

next_steps.description: Add error checking and retry to read_temperature in sensor.c
Add error constant and error check to get_temperature_celsius

arguments.title: I2C sensor read error handling pattern
arguments.content: Always check i2c_read return value. On failure, bus lines go high → 0xFF bytes → garbage readings like 0xFFFF or 0x8000. Pattern: retry up to 3 times, validate raw value isn't all-1s, return error sentinel on failure.

turns.content: Finding recorded: I2C sensor read error handling pattern

arguments.summary: Fixed garbage sensor readings by adding I2C error checking with 3 retries and 0xFFFF validation. get_temperature_celsius returns -999.0f on persistent failure.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed intermittent garbage sensor readings. Added I2C error checking, 3-retry mechanism, and 0xFFFF value validation. On persistent failure, returns -999.0f sentinel value instead of garbage.
